title: Commas and introductory elements
description: Basically, you can use commas at the beginning of sentences to set up an introduction.
topic_path: Punctuation: end-of-sentence punctuation, the comma, and the apostrophe

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the correct placement for commas in this sentence.**

Exhausted the basketball team boarded the shuttle home.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Introductory elements** come at the start of a sentence and provide useful descriptive information. We should *use a comma* to separate introductory elements from the rest of the sentence.

For example:

*  _Baring its teeth, the dog growled fiercely._ 
* _First, measure three cups of flour._

"Exhausted" is an introductory element, so we should separate it from the rest of the sentence with a comma:

>**Exhausted, the basketball team boarded the shuttle home.**

choices.content: Exhausted$\redD{,}$ the basketball team boarded the shuttle home.
Exhausted$\redD{,}$ the basketball team$\redD{,}$ boarded the shuttle home.
Exhausted the basketball team$\redD{,}$ boarded the shuttle home.
